titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/73 (1)

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=690 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:14:10.666384+00 | translation moa=691 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:14:16.161409+00

div.p:
Vniuersis ecclesie filiis presens scriptum uisuris uel audituris Walterus filius Alani Senescaldus domini Regis Scocie Salutem . Nouerit
uniuersitas uestra quod ego honori dei et paci ecclesie . et maxime quieti de Melros uolens prouidere  dubia quondam contenta in
Carta Walteri aui mei data Monachis de Melros duxi declaranda . In illa parte scilicet ipsius Carte ubi dictus auus meus loquitur
de landis et de halches et de pastura foreste . volo etiam hoc quod ibi minus caritatis et securitatis continetur  In hoc instrv
mento clarius exprimere . et dictis Monachis plenius prouidere . Concessi igitur prefatis Monachis omnes landas et halches tam in fo
resta mea quam extra ex aquilonari parte fluminis de Ar et totam forestam meam ad utendum secundum quod eis utiliuis uisum fuerit . scilicet
ad arandum et seminandum ubicumque sibi uiderint expedire  tam in foresta quam extra . et ad nutriendum et pascendum et
habendum in eadem foresta assidue pecora sua quotquot uoluerint secundum quod territorium eiusdem foreste poterit continere . et hoc
erit in arbitrio Monachorum estimare quantum idem territorium poterit sustinere . Concessi etiam eis usum eiusdem foreste in
omnibus aisiamentis et utilitatibus ipsius habendum et possidendum plene et pacifice ad omnes necessitates suas hoc solo excepto
quod Monachi nec conuersi nec aliqui alii illorum auctoritate  uenabuntur nec accipitres capient in eadem foresta hoc
enim illorum ordini non conuenit nec illis credimus expedire . Hec omnia deo et prefatis Monachis in perpetuum dedi con
cessi et hac mea carta confirmaui . Ego quidem et heredes et successores mei uniuersi warantizabimus et defendemus
predictis Monachis omnia supradicta et eos illa faciemus in pace possidere libere et quiete ab omni seruitio et exactione et con
suetudine seculari . Et ut hec mea concessio firma sit et inperpetuum illesa presens scriptum prefatis Monachis Sigillo
meo presenti tradidi roboratum . Hiis testibus . Willelmo capellano meo . Malcolmo filio comitis de leuenaus nepo
te meo . Iohanne de mundegumeri . Roger filio Glay . Willelmo de hauekerest’ . Alano Wallensi . Vmfirdo de Bosco .
Iohanne de lindesey . Hugone filio Simonis filii Bertini . et Multis Aliis .

p.cl: To all sons of the Church who shall view or hear the present writing, Walter son of Alan, steward of the king of Scotland, sends greeting.

I, in honour of God and for the peace of the Church, and mainly wishing to provide tranquility at Melrose, have thought fit to clarify doubts once contained in the charter of Walter my grandfather given to the monks of Melrose, in that part of the charter, that is, where my said grandfather spoke of untilled lands and haughs and of forest pasture. This I also will, that where there is contained less of charity and security, in this instrument to describe more clearly, and to provide for the monks more fully.

I have therefore granted to the aforementioned monks all untilled lands and haughs, as well in my forests as outside, on the northern side of the River Ayr, and my whole forest to be used according to what should seem the more useful to them, that is, to be tilled and sown wherever it should seem to them to be suitable, as well within the forest as outside, and for the feeding and pasturing and constantly keeping their flocks within the same forest, as many as they wish, according to what the territory of the same forest is able to contain; and it shall be in the judgment of the monks to calculate how much the same land is able to support. I have also granted to them the use of the same forest in all its easements and benefits; to be had and possessed fully and peaceably for all their own needs; this alone excepted, that [neither] the monks nor the lay-brothers nor any other of their men under authority shall hunt or catch sparrow-hawks in the same forest; for this is not appropriate to their order, not do we believe it to be beneficial for them. All these things have I given, granted, and made firm by this my charter, to God and the aforementioned monks. And I, and my heirs and successors all, shall warrant and defend for the aforesaid monks all the above-said things and we shall cause them to possess them in peace, free and quit from every service and exaction and secular custom.
And so that my grant should be firm and unimpaired, I have handed over the present writing confirmed with my own present seal.

With these witnesses: William, my chaplain; Malcolm, son of the earl of Lennox, my nephew; John of Montgomery; Roger son of Glay; William of Halkerston; Alan Wallace; Humphrey del Bois; John of Lindsay; Hugh, son of Simon son of Bertin; and many others.
